chapters.title: Let your inner voice "yoke" your body
chapters.text:
For the first time, Jessamyn Stanley heard a voice that prompted her to follow her yoga path after a series of personal tragedies. She was already practicing yoga but didn't take it seriously, seeing it as just a hobby. One day at home, she heard an internal voice — her own, but remarkably confident and insistent — telling her she had the strength to practice yoga independently.

Inspired, she went to her yoga mat and began practicing the iconic Bikram Yoga breath, finding the experience of studying without supervision exhilarating. As she practiced, she noticed a stark difference between public and private sessions; at home, she felt sincere, outspoken, and free. Rather than worrying about unfamiliar techniques, Stanley focused on applying her existing knowledge.

Yoga is a beautiful gesture of cultural appreciation if you let your body and mind learn more.

The word "yoke" means to unite. It's about balance — something everyone needs these days. Who knows — perhaps the solution to your problem lies in the downward-facing dog pose? Why not give it a try after you finish here?

chapters.title: Build a bridge pose between you and internal peace
chapters.text:
After hearing that inner voice ten years ago, Stanley became an author and even participated in campaigns for Amazon and Adidas. Still, she often faced discrimination even after gaining success — something no one is prepared for when it appears.

Stanley was supposed to be the cover hero of Yoga Journal, but when she went to buy the magazine, she saw another woman on the cover — it turned out that the magazine had two different covers.

As a woman of color, she saw this action as proof of racism. Many US yoga teachers ignore the reality of racism, as American yoga is a predominantly white practice. Stanley is also candid about her body in her work and admits that she doesn't feel attractive when losing weight.

A happy or unhappy life is your own creation … You are your own best friend as well as your worst enemy. ~ Satchidananda Saraswati

Jessamyn Stanley

Stanley confessed that she had faced discrimination and harassment, but now she doesn't shy away from feeling weak or hurt.

Yoga can provide many enlightening experiences if you practice long enough.

At the heart of Stanley's practice is the yoga of everyday life. You don't need to excel at South Asian culture to gain a deeper understanding of yoga, but it's important to remember that classical yoga differs from the American version. The second draws on a combination of Hinduism, Buddhism, Jainism, and other spiritual and cultural traditions, with roots going back to the early 20th century when renowned teachers came to the United States from India.

The yoga of daily life is about finding flexibility and vitality even in life's worst moments. It's finding strength after loss, patience in parenting, and mindfulness during stress. Yoga means maintaining balance and awareness through life's constant changes and staying grounded no matter what.

chapters.title: Faith is the essential asana
chapters.text:
A family friend once accidentally pointed out that Stanley's mother was drinking an alcoholic cocktail. It was an eye-opening moment for young Stanley — she realized that parents have their own hidden truths. The experience made Stanley reflect on how everyone wears masks. Words and behaviors often conceal something more, yet beneath these facades lies a universal essence that unites us all.

Yoga exposes the edges of this mask. It can introduce you to your true self, hidden beneath the surface. Don't hide your inner light; live as if no one is looking at you.

Stanley followed the Bahá'í religion during her childhood and adolescence. Members of this religion follow these rules:

• Treat people the way you would like them to treat you.
• Men and women are equal.
• Racial equality is essential — Bahá'ís value communication and marriage between people of different races.

Although only in her teens, she understood what it meant to be a Bahá'í — trying your best to be perfect. However, due to adolescent hormones, the issue of premarital chastity was quickly set aside. Moreover, the religion's views on LGBTQ+ issues clashed with Stanley's emerging queer identity. Unwilling to feel ashamed of herself, she left the Bahá'í Faith at 20.

Leaving the faith drove Stanley to redefine sacred texts for herself. She found inspiration in authors like James Baldwin, Gary Zukav, Don Miguel Ruiz, and Dr. Maya Angelou. More specifically, Swami Satchidananda's translation of the Patanjali Yoga Sutras — 196 Sanskrit aphorisms about yoga — was remarkably influential. In other words, she built a trustworthy knowledge base to deepen her craft; you can do the same by finding a person or idea that inspires you to do your best.

You might not follow any faith, but there is always truth inside you.

Did you know? The poses now recognized as hallmarks of American yoga blend classical yoga postures, European gymnastics, Indian weightlifting, and various kinesthetic movements that gained popularity in the 19th and 20th centuries.

chapters.title: Start with taking a seat
chapters.text:
When Stanley followed the Bahá'í Faith, she observed a strict fast for the 19 days before the vernal equinox. Hunger and thirst sharpened her focus on the present moment; as it turns out, yoga does the same.

Fasting revealed to Stanley that she tended to complain, feel smug, and enjoy projecting her emotions onto others. While not everyone expects to confront their shadow during a simple physical activity, it's crucial to be open to such encounters, even when unexpected. Your darker aspects are as much a part of you as your kindness or empathy are. So accepting them is an act of self-honesty, which is inherently honorable.

For many, yoga is primarily seen as a physical activity rather than a discipline focused on breathing or consciousness. In American yoga, poses are central. These postures introduce the fundamentals of yoga but are ultimately a means to achieve inner understanding. The spiritual path begins with learning proper breathing and developing spirituality. However, the most significant journey in life is the ability to grow old, as aging infuses life with meaning and depth.

While poses are not the most crucial part of yoga, they're essential to your training. Still, you don't need to perform complex elements; there's only one posture you must master — sitting quietly. You can try it right now and see if it's trickier than it sounds.

The most challenging yoga poses are the best teachers because they free you from obstacles to spirituality.

To deepen your meditation, focus entirely on your body and observe how it responds. In each pose, notice your thoughts about the position of your head and how your heart beats. Remember to breathe mindfully and correctly. Be aware of the slightest tension, even if it's a new facial wrinkle. Savor the moment when you feel that your face is finally relaxed. And above all, ignore others' comments about your posture.

If you commit to the path of yoga, you'll always end up seeing yourself… Every superpower you seek is already happening inside of you. ~ Jessamyn Stanley

Jessamyn Stanley

chapters.title: How social media leveled astrology and yoga
chapters.text:
Learning yoga in the Instagram era has become more of a performance. Nowadays, yoga spirituality heavily relies on celebrity culture. People seek power and recognition, so they follow those who have already achieved these. Yet, yoga reminds us that all searches begin from within.

While capitalism tells us to look beyond ourselves, yoga says the opposite. However, capitalism is why yoga has become so popular and widespread worldwide. In America, yoga culture focuses on materialism and consumerism rather than spirituality. Stanley describes wealth as the leading American religion, with consumerism permeating even the yoga world.

However, many people don't think about money when starting their yoga practice; they might become yoga teachers later. In the process of knowing themselves, people realize that transcendence goes beyond the power of money. After all, connection with spiritual power has nothing to do with someone's poverty or wealth.

Teaching yoga in the Instagram era means constantly battling your ego. It involves regular feed updates, planning posts and stories, and daily content creation. Stanley reminds herself not to confuse her true self with her social media image, as avatars are often the best versions of the user. She adds that even online yoga can be valuable, as it still conveys the same truth — the importance of self-care.

As capitalism embraced yoga, you can test your integrity by questioning whether your approach is genuine or forced.

Stanley once relied heavily on logic, but two paths helped her better understand her spirit. The first was using tarot cards, which she was introduced to by her aunt. Tarot allows Stanley to tap into her intuition when logic fails and she's unsure of her next move. Astrology, the spiritual extension of astronomy, is the second path. While many are skeptical of astrology, horoscopes often provoke thought. Astrology doesn't predict the future but can offer insights about the present. For Stanley, the allure of astrology serves as a reminder that humanity and the galaxy are composed of the same fundamental particles.

chapters.title: Harness your energy like an airbender
chapters.text:
Stanley firmly believes that yoga is not possible without careful breathing control. Breathing connects you to the life force within you and the world around you. You're already doing yoga when you breathe, regardless of the pose. While breath control can be challenging, it's essential to establish a strong connection with your physical body first.

Prana is a cosmic energy that constantly flows through us. When you're sick or hurt, the prana around you is out of balance. But you can control the prana and its interactions with your body. With practice, you can control your nerve endings and visualize your thoughts and emotions flowing.

Pranayama is the regulation of prana cosmic energy. For instance, by holding your breath, you control the oxygen flow into your body, a vital component of life. In turn, this regulates your physical body's functions and corrects prana imbalances.

Pranayama can be practiced alone or in groups; it gives you control over the energy flowing through you and all living things. While it primarily involves breath control exercises, pranayama goes far beyond that. Prana fills the space and resides within physical and spiritual realms, surrounding and connecting all living beings beyond their breath.

Yoga regulates your physical and spiritual energy — a unique combination of activities you can practice at home.

If your yoga teacher doesn't emphasize breathing, work on establishing a connection with it yourself. Any comfortable posture is suitable for pranayama. Sit straight, straighten your chest, relax your shoulders, and imagine a crown on your head pointing toward the sky (that is, keep your head straight).

Stanley recommends practicing pranayama regardless of your physical posture. Find a comfortable and stable position. She suggests starting with even breaths through the nose. You can also work on inhaling and exhaling for specific counts.

chapters.title: Don’t fear your thoughts
chapters.text:
Meditation is the practice of contemplation. Many people mistakenly believe you must close your eyes during meditation. Truth be told, you can practice meditation whenever you want because every moment of life is meditation.

Stanley recalls when her thoughts and subconscious mind overwhelmed her during meditation. If you find yourself in a similar situation, don't try to suppress your thoughts. Let them flow naturally. Most of the difficulty of meditation comes from the desperate desire to create an idealized image of meditation. You don't need to seek calmness — silence is an illusion and exists within chaos.

Everyone inherently understands meditation from birth because children meditate all day long. Reflection and observation of the world are a child's primary activities. Growing up, most people learn to listen to others more than to themselves. Meditation can be a way to reconnect with your inner child and the version of yourself that circumstances may have pushed aside long ago. In other words, when you immerse yourself in meditation, you soothe that small, vulnerable part of you that is afraid to show up before the world.

Our thoughts are chaotic, and meditation is the ability to accept the chaos.

Stanley began her meditation practice by simply sitting in silence. Try it yourself, though it may be more challenging than it seems, as the thoughts you try to delay can trap you in their cycle. You can meditate with closed or open eyes, according to your preference. Stanley focuses on inhaling and exhaling through the nose. She also sets a timer to take her mind off the concept of time. Also, the length of the meditation doesn't matter. All you need to do is be present in your existence.

You can also try meditating with music — Stanley honors her ancestors by singing traditional songs. Music is prana, the energy that contains dreams, sorrow, and love. It can lead you to a deeper connection with your breathing, though it might be distracting initially. So, consider crafting your yoga playlist once you've learned to play your inner instrument perfectly.

chapters.title: Conclusion
chapters.text:
In the Internet age, the general perception of yoga has become somewhat distorted. Yoga enables you to connect your inner self with the world. The more powerful this bond is, the firmer your stance becomes, both on the mat and in life.

If you commit to reaching this milestone, surrendering to the stream of thoughts and accepting their chaos could be the most rewarding surrender in the long run. Still, the most essential aspect of yoga is being yourself. Find the courage to make mistakes and move forward. Be your own instructor and let the world teach you.

Try this

• To warm up for your yoga session, surround yourself with calming or visually pleasing items, such as crystals, candles, or pictures.
• If you don’t know what feelings you want to release, carry a notebook and jot down your thoughts throughout the day.

Read it till the end?
